title: Castanea sumatrana
intro: Castanea sumatrana, also known as the Sumatran chestnut tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fagaceae, native to Indonesia and Malaysia. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Castanea sumatrana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Castanea sumatrana is commonly prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Castanea sumatrana is a species of chestnut tree native to Indonesia. It belongs to the family Fagaceae. The genus Castanea is a group of deciduous trees in the Fagaceae family, commonly known as chestnut trees. Castanea sumatrana is often classified alongside other species in the Castanea genus, which includes several species of edible chestnuts. The exact classification and relationships within the Castanea genus are still being researched and refined by botanists.
uses: Castanea sumatrana, a species of chestnut tree native to Southeast Asia, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits. The bark, leaves, and seeds of the tree are rich in antioxidants and other bioactive compounds that have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. In traditional medicine, Castanea sumatrana is used to treat various ailments, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections. The tree's extracts have also been studied for their potential to inhibit the growth of certain cancer cells and to protect against cardiovascular disease. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Castanea sumatrana and to standardize its use in modern medicine.

parts: Castanea sumatrana is a species of chestnut tree native to Sumatra, Indonesia.
